overall.description: A first-person view from the bridge of a cargo vessel navigating through rough seas during dusk or dawn. The scene features operational navigation monitors displaying radar and chart data, with the ship's bow cutting through choppy waters under grey overcast skies. The footage appears to be from a maritime ship simulator game.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge interior with navigation consoles overlooking the bow

agents.ego_description: piloting the vessel from the bridge, monitoring the radar and navigation displays while the ship maintains steady course through rough seas

entered.detail: Navigation display showing chart data with cyan blue coloring
Circular radar screen with rotating yellow sweep arm and concentric range rings
Depth sounder display showing orange and blue gradient readings
Red-painted forecastle deck with white railings and anchor equipment visible through the bridge windows
Choppy dark blue sea with visible wave crests extending to the horizon

segments.scene_description: The view from inside a ship's bridge shows three navigation monitors at the top of the frame displaying charts, radar with active rotating sweep, and depth data. Through the forward windows, the vessel's bow deck is visible cutting through rough, choppy waters under an overcast dusk sky. The ship maintains steady forward motion throughout the clip with no other vessels or obstacles present.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot maintains a steady first-person perspective from a ship's bridge as it navigates through a desolate, choppy sea under a dim sky. It begins with a focus on the interior bridge equipment, where three navigation monitors display active radar sweeps, nautical charts, and depth data at the top of the frame. As the camera looks forward, the view extends through the bridge windows to the vessel's bow cutting through the rough, grey waters. The scene continues with the ship maintaining a constant forward trajectory, pushing deeper into the overcast dusk environment. Finally, the camera remains fixed on the rhythmic motion of the bow as it clears the open, obstacle-free sea.
